content: Management differs fundamentally: Physical dependence requires gradual tapering to prevent withdrawal (opioid taper 10% per week; benzodiazepine taper over months using long-acting agent; beta-blocker taper over 1-2 weeks to prevent rebound hypertension/tachycardia). Addiction treatment involves: detoxification (managed withdrawal), rehabilitation (behavioral therapy, counseling), medication-assisted treatment (methadone/buprenorphine for opioid use disorder; naltrexone for alcohol/opioid; acamprosate for alcohol), long-term recovery support. Opioid example: cancer patient on chronic opioids has physical dependence - continue opioids, taper slowly if discontinuing. Patient seeking early refills, using multiple prescribers, IV use has addiction - refer for addiction treatment, consider MAT. Stigma reduction: avoid terms like 'abuse,' 'addict'; use 'substance use disorder,' 'person with addiction.'
